regime.note: B5 ✓ (HY OAS 317bp <350, shrinking). B4 △ (Net Liq +0.6% WoW, TGA drawdown but not yet 4wk MA rising). B1 ✗ (real rates 2.02%, still elevated despite -4bp). B2 ✗ (DXY 99.90, 4wk MA flat/up). B3 ✗ (JPY 159.67, override +7.1% from 3/20). Mechanical: 🔴 Contraction (1.5/5). Mitigated to 🟡 by L7=0.20 🟢, L8=0 🟢, HY calm, MOVE rising but sub-crisis.
regime.quadrant_basis: ISM 52.4 (>50 = growth up) + Core PCE 3.05% (>2.5% = inflation up)
regime.dominant_path: Normal 1 (Fed path) — pause mode, hike risk persisting. Dot plot 3.4% end-2026.
regime.disruption_1: Disruption 1 (yen carry) PARTIALLY ACTIVE 🟡 — USD/JPY 159.67 (+7.1% from 3/20 base) but VIX 27.44 <30. CFTC JPY shorts -67.8K (20-month high). Crowded carry.
regime.disruption_2: Disruption 2 (oil) PARTIALLY ACTIVE 🟡 — Brent $100.70 (+42.2% 30d) but BEI +3bp only (2.34%). Trump pause on Iran strikes → price retreat from $112 peak. Hormuz still disrupted. Qatar LNG damage.
regime.disruption_3: Disruption 3 (fiscal) PARTIALLY ACTIVE 🟡 — Term premium 0.87% (StreetStats) + B1 real rate 2.02%. D8 +24bp but B1 only +22bp (from 1.80% on 3/20). Not yet simultaneous threshold (+30bp D8 + +20bp B1).
regime.keystone: Core PCE 3.05% — Fed hike territory (>2.5%). Keystone REVERSE direction. Inflation re-acceleration.
regime.narrative: Good news = Bad — strong PMI 52.4 + sticky inflation = rate hike bets. FedWatch: 94.8% hold April, >50% pricing at least one hike 2026.

A1.name: Core PCE YoY
A1.keystone_state: REVERSE — A1 >2.5%, Fed hike territory. Inflation re-acceleration from 2.4% to 3.05% YoY.

A2.name: China Credit Impulse
A2.value: CNY 5.61T (Jan+Feb cumulative), Feb 1.01T CNY lowest since 2020
A2.detail: PBOC loan prime rate unchanged 10 months. Government borrowing props headline. Private demand weak. Bad loans rising across major lenders. PBOC pushing state banks into stimulus mode. Credit directed, not demand-driven.

B1.name: Real Rate 10Y TIPS
B1.verdict: B1 ✗ — 2.02%, -4bp from prev 2.06% (3/26), but +22bp from 1.80% (3/20 base). 4wk trend still rising. Not -10bp decline threshold.

B2.name: DXY
B2.verdict: B2 ✗ — DXY 99.90, +0.1% DoD, +2.5% from 3/20 base. 4wk MA turning up. Dollar strengthening on safe haven + hike bets.

B3.name: USD/JPY
B3.verdict: B3 ✗ — 159.67, +7.2% from 3/20 base. Override threshold (±3%) BREACHED and sustained. CFTC JPY shorts -67.8K (20-month high, from -41.4K prior week). Disruption 1 partially active.

B4.name: Net Liquidity
B4.verdict: B4 △ — $5.780T, +0.6% from $5.746T (3/26). TGA drawdown from $910B to $876B is the driver. Not yet 4wk MA rising trend confirmed. Borderline.
B4.source: FRED WALCL + CEIC TGA + FRED RRP

B5.name: HY OAS
B5.verdict: B5 ✓ — 317bp, -2bp. Below 350bp threshold + shrinking trend. Credit market calm despite geopolitical stress, MOVE surge, and rate fears.

summary.detail: B5 ✓ only. B4 △ (borderline, +0.6% but 4wk not confirmed). B1 ✗ (+22bp from base, elevated). B2 ✗ (DXY +2.5%). B3 ✗ (JPY override +7.2%).
summary.mechanical_regime: 🔴 Contraction (1.5/5 per RULES.md §1 → round to 1 or 2 → 🔴 if 1, 🟡 if 2)
summary.override_note: L7=0.20 🟢, L8=0 🟢. No systemic stress. HY 317bp calm. MOVE 115 rising but sub-crisis. Mechanical 🔴 but qualitative factors + B4 △ favor 🟡 Transition.

C1.name: VIX
C1.cross_check: B5 mismatch ⚠ — VIX 27.44 elevated vs HY 317bp calm
C1.detail: VIX 27.44 (elevated, 6th consecutive day >25). Geopolitical fear (Iran-Israel, Hormuz) + rate uncertainty vs credit calm.
C1.L7_acute_check: VIX >25 for 5+ consecutive days. L7 acute trigger monitoring active.

C2.name: MOVE Index
C2.cross_check: B1 partial alignment — MOVE surging with rate uncertainty. Bond vol rising sharply.
C2.detail: MOVE 115.02, up +17.4 from 97.59 (3/26). Reversal of post-FOMC calm. Bond market volatility spiking on Iran escalation + hike repricing. L7 MOVE component rising (0.292 vs 0.147 prev).

C3.name: T10Y2Y
C3.cross_check: B1 consistent — curve flattening continues as short end rises faster

C4.name: US M2
C4.cross_check: B4 partial — M2 expanding but Net Liq recovery nascent (TGA drain offset)

C5.name: ISM PMI
C5.cross_check: A1 consistent — expansion + inflation pressure (prices paid surging)
C5.detail: Feb 2026. New orders 55.8. Employment slowing to 8mo low. Supplier delivery times lengthened to Oct 2022 levels. March release pending ~Apr 1.

C6.name: Unemployment Rate
C6.cross_check: A1 consistent — not yet 5% keystone trigger. 4.7%+ 2mo watch.

C7.name: Brent Crude
C7.cross_check: A1 mismatch ⚠ — oil $100+ sustains inflation pressure. Disruption 2 partially active.
C7.note: Iran-Israel conflict ongoing. Strait of Hormuz disrupted. Trump paused Iran energy strikes 3/23 → price retreated from $112 peak. Qatar Ras Laffan LNG damaged ($20B/yr loss). Brent now consolidating $99-101.

C8.name: BEI 10Y Breakeven
C8.cross_check: B1 partial — inflation expectations ticking up. Oil sustaining BEI pressure.
C8.detail: BEI 2.34%, +3bp. Oil shock beginning to feed into inflation expectations. Disruption 2 pathway showing activation: Brent $100 → BEI +3bp.

C9.name: USD/CNY
C9.cross_check: A2 consistent — yuan stable despite credit weakness and PBOC rate hold

C10.name: Fed Funds Rate
C10.cross_check: B1 consistent — rate on hold at 3.50-3.75% range
C10.detail: FOMC 3/18 held. Dot plot: 3.4% end 2026. FedWatch 3/26: 94.8% hold for April, ~75% hold full year, >50% pricing at least one hike 2026.

summary.detail: C1 VIX 27.44 vs B5 HY 317bp — geopolitical fear vs credit calm (sustained mismatch). C7 Brent $100.70 vs A1 Core PCE 3.05% — oil → inflation pressure, disruption 2 partially active.
summary.new_signal: ⚠ C2 MOVE 115.02 surging (+17.4 from 97.59). Bond vol spike. L7 MOVE component 0.292 (from 0.147). Watch for sustained L7 rise.
summary.note: C8 BEI +3bp — first sign of oil shock feeding into inflation expectations. Disruption 2 pathway activating: Brent $100 → BEI ↑.

D2.name: Bank Reserves
D2.note: ⚠⚠ Bank reserves $2.994T — BREACHED critical $3.0T threshold. First time below $3T. Short-term liquidity crunch early warning ACTIVE. Watch for money market stress, SOFR spikes.

D3.name: Reverse Repo (RRP)
D3.note: RRP fully drained. Residual fluctuation around zero. No longer provides liquidity tailwind.

D4.name: TGA Balance
D4.note: TGA declined from $910B to $876B. Tax season inflows winding down, government spending resuming. Positive for Net Liquidity.

D6.name: SOFR
D6.note: Aligned with DFF at 3.64%. SOFR-DFF gap = 0bp. L7 SOFR component = 0. Despite D2 reserves breach, no SOFR stress yet.

D7.name: Fiscal Deficit/GDP
D7.detail: CBO Feb 2026: 5.8% in FY2026. $1T deficit in first 5 months. Structural. US declared insolvent by GAO (29th consecutive disclaimer). $47.8T liabilities + $88.4T unfunded = $136.2T total.

D8.name: Term Premium (ACM 10Y)
D8.note: StreetStats composite 0.87% (3/20). ACM model prior reading 0.74%. Rising — fiscal dominance pressure + geopolitical risk premium.

D9.name: CFTC JPY Net Position
D9.note: JPY shorts surged to -67.8K (from -41.4K). Largest weekly USD buying ($11B) since June 2018. Gross shorts +20.2% in one week. Yen carry trade at crowding risk. MOF/BOJ intervention talk rising.
D9.source: CFTC COT via Tavily (Investing.com, InvestMacro, Saxo Bank)

D10.name: FedWatch
D10.detail: FOMC 3/18 held at 3.50-3.75%. Dot plot: 3.4% end 2026. FedWatch 3/26: 94.8% hold April. >50% pricing at least one hike before year-end. Major shift from prior -50bp cut expectation.
D10.change: April hold near-certainty. Hike probability rising.
D10.source: Tavily (CME FedWatch via TechFlow, ADM, MEXC)
